testPurpose.objective: Verify four isolated natural users can create and join a Love Letter room, retain fixed identities, select a visible non-default one-heart target, complete the full match through legal UI actions without forced card strategy, preserve all private hands and actor-only results, and reach one cross-tab-consistent terminal result.

successCriteria.description: All four tabs retain distinct fixed identities from room entry through terminal.
The host selects one heart instead of the visible four-player default of four, and every tab visibly agrees.
Protected, eliminated, waiting, or otherwise restricted players cannot perform actions absent from their own visible UI.
Exact hands, draws, pending choices, and actor-only results never appear on non-owner tabs or in non-owner Observations.
Public cards, player states, heart totals, and current player agree across all four tabs.
All four tabs normalize the full one-heart match to the same result.

testPurpose.recommendationRationale: One heart is a legal visible value and differs from the four-player UI default of four. It minimizes repetitive rounds while retaining a genuine complete match, natural card decisions, multi-player targeting, protection/elimination checks, and production server timing.
